title: scoparia montevidensis var. neglecta
intro: Scoparia montevidensis var. neglecta, commonly known as Japanese lemon balm or poor man's lemon balm, is a perennial herb in the Lamiaceae family, native to East Asia. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its antispasmodic, anti-inflammatory, and antiviral properties, making it a potential remedy for various health issues. The health benefits of Scoparia montevidensis var. neglecta include reducing stress and anxiety, alleviating menstrual cramps, and soothing digestive issues. It can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or infused oil, and is often consumed to reap its medicinal properties and experience its calming effects.
